title: Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:Missions17.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 17:
Darth Vader's Return'' |author=[[Ryder Windham]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[January]] [[1999]]''[[Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=72 |isbn=059084413X |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]''[[Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return]]'' and the next three ''Star Wars Missions'' books tell a continuous story that starts with Darth Vader returning to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Though ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that Vader did not return to Coruscant until he had discovered Luke Skywalker's identity, [[Empire 14|''Empire'' 14]] depicts Vader returning immediately to Coruscant after crashing on Vaal, a fact which is repeated in ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]''. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Missions 16: Imperial Jailbreak]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Missions 18: Rogue Squadron to the Rescue]]'' }} '''''Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return''''' is the seventeenth adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[January]] [[1999]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''Darth Vader has returned to Coruscant after the destruction of the first Death Star. There, he discovers his next mission. In the days before Grand Moff Tarkin's death, Tarkin recorded a top secret holotape... complete with information that would enable the Empire to dominate hyperspace. Your mission: to retrieve the missing holotape and bring glory to the Emperor.'' ''Good luck.'' ==Plot summary== [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] arrives on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] in a [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruiser]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. He orders that [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mordak]] have his ship. Meanwhile, in deep space, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Termo]] and [[Communications officer/Legends|Communications officer]] [[Tix]] have spent weeks in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], and are about to make planetfall on an uncharted planet. Vader meets with his master, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who stipulates that he not move against [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin IV]]. Termo and Tix crash on the planet and locate a transmission relay tower. They contact Imperial Center, and Palpatine dispatches Vader, Mordak, a [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]] and several TIE pilots to the uncharted planet. There, they are to retrieve [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Tarkin's]] secret [[Hologram disc/Legends|holotape]] from Termo. Upon reaching the planet, the Imperials are attacked by a droid ship. One of the group gets to his [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] craft, and land on the planet below, but the ''Carrack'' is ensnared in the droid ship's grasp. Encountering Termo's escape pod, the Imperial ventures further through the planet, searching for the elusive Admiral. Spotting the relay tower, the Imperial journeys towards it, and investigates the interior. While at the tower, the Imperial frees the ''Carrack'' from the tractor beam, then rescues Termo and Tix, before meeting with the ''Carrack''. Vader, displeased, executes Termo, and retrieves the holotape. He orders three [[TIE pilot]]s to remain at the planet and destroy the relay tower, to prevent it falling into [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hands. The floating droid inhabitants of the planet, in a panic, contact their "brethren," [[Q-7N]], who has joined the Rebels on Yavin 4. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars
